query: Create a brief for a JWT authentication endpoint. It's for our REST API, developers need it to get tokens, we're using Node.js with Express, tokens expire after 1h, refresh tokens last 7d.

context.preconditions: No prior artifacts exist. Rich upfront context is provided by the user including tech stack, token lifetimes, and target audience.

expected_behavior: Skill confirms feature name (e.g., 'JWT Authentication Endpoint') and proceeds section by section, asking fewer questions due to rich initial context
Skill drafts an Executive Summary covering what the feature is (JWT auth endpoint), who it serves (developers), and why it matters
Skill produces a Problem Statement with 3+ pain points, a framing sentence, and a closing consequence sentence, then asks if the user has verbatims
Skill generates a Solution section with a bolded core design principle, 3-7 Key Objectives, 5-10 In Scope items, and 3-8 Out of Scope items
Skill writes BRIEF.md to docs/features/jwt-auth-endpoint/BRIEF.md with all 8 template sections and no placeholders

query: I need a brief for notifications.

context.preconditions: User provides a single word for the feature with no details about type of notifications, target audience, tech stack, or problem being solved.

expected_behavior: Skill asks an open question to gather missing context: 'Describe the notification feature — what it does, who it is for, and any constraints'
Skill identifies specific gaps in Problem Statement and Success Criteria sections and asks targeted follow-up questions for each
Skill never fills a section with vague or generic content — it asks rather than guessing when information is insufficient
Skill works through all sections sequentially, proposing a draft of each and asking for confirmation before moving on

query: Create a brief for enterprise auth gaps.

context.preconditions: A validated PROBLEM-FRAME.md exists with a chosen framing about identity infrastructure gaps blocking enterprise adoption, root cause of SMB-era auth architecture, and constraints including backward compatibility. A DECISION.md with accepted decisions also exists.

expected_behavior: Skill detects and reads the existing PROBLEM-FRAME.md and DECISION.md before gathering context
Skill informs the user: 'I found a PROBLEM-FRAME.md for this feature. I will use the validated problem statement and constraints as the foundation for the brief.'
Skill derives Problem Statement pain points from the problem frame's root cause analysis, seeds Out of Scope from problem boundaries, and seeds Success Criteria from measurable signals
Skill asks fewer questions because the problem frame answers most of them — focuses on Solution, Objectives, and In Scope which the problem frame does not cover
Skill treats all accepted decisions from DECISION.md as hard constraints and does not re-open them
